title: Halimione fera
taxonomy: Halimione fera is classified under the family Amaranthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as amaranths or pigweeds. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower arrangement. Halimione fera is further classified under the genus Halimione, which consists of several species of salt-tolerant plants. The species Halimione fera is a type of succulent plant that is native to certain regions with salty or brackish environments. It is often found growing in coastal areas or along rivers and estuaries.

The plant is said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial for treating various ailments. intro: Halimione portulacoides, commonly known as Halimione or Sea Purslane, is a succulent halophyte plant native to the Mediterranean region and the Middle East. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Halimione portulacoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Halimione is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to make tinctures and ointments.
